_comment: Declarative invariant battery for evolve(focus='invariants'). Add new checks as JSON — no Python changes needed.

_types.files_executable: All files matching glob (minus exclude) must be executable
_types.files_referenced: All files matching glob must appear in reference_file. match_mode='stem' strips extension before matching
_types.symlink_valid: Symlink at path must resolve to existing target
_types.json_valid: File at path must be valid JSON
_types.glob_count_gte: Count of files matching glob (minus exclude) must be >= min_count
_types.pattern_in_file: Regex pattern must match somewhere in file at path
_types.patterns_all_in_file: All patterns (array) must match somewhere in file at path
_types.pattern_count_gte: Count of regex matches in file must be >= min_count
_types.symbols_used: Every symbol matching definition_pattern in definition_file must appear in files matching usage_glob (usage_pattern template with {symbol} placeholder)
_types.symbols_have_kb: The top_n highest-caller IIFE globals (with >= min_callers callers) must each have at least one KB entry
_types.files_mtime_window: Two files (path_a and path_b or path_b_glob for latest match) must have mtimes within max_delta_seconds of each other
_types.kb_content_no_pattern: Scan all KB entries; fail if any title or content matches the given regex pattern (guards against LLM artifact leaks)
_types.kb_freshness: Warn if no KB entry has been updated within max_age_days days (staleness signal)
_types.shell_output_empty: Run shell command; pass if stdout is empty, fail if any output produced. Use for git-clean checks.
_types.metric_has_variance: A pipeline-metric JSON file's field must have >= min_distinct values across its recent history. Catches silent zero-valued-metric cascades where the whole observability substrate reports null/0 because one upstream input is broken.
_types.correlation_direction: A named correlation's sign must match expectation (positive|negative) with minimum |r|. The ultimate anti-drift check: detects when HME's self-metric is actively ANTI-correlated with musical outcome — i.e., HME thinks it's improving while the music gets worse. Fires the 'optimization direction is wrong' alarm.
_types.activity_events_balanced: Paired activity events (start_event + end_event) must fire with matching counts in the recent window. Catches observability regressions where a refactor drops one of a paired emission silently.
_types.activity_field_sanity: A named field on recent activity events must match an expected regex. Catches garbage-value bugs (e.g. file_written emitting module='<UUID>' from infrastructure files instead of real module basenames).
_types.same_commit_determinism: Consecutive pipeline runs from the SAME commit must produce metric values within tolerance. Non-determinism means either random inputs (clock-dependent verifier) or cached state leaking between runs — both invalidate the metric.
_types.invariant_chronically_failing: Escalation: another invariant has been FAILing for N consecutive runs. Catches the meta-pattern where alarms become background noise and never get fixed.

invariants.description: Every hook script must be executable

Every TOP-LEVEL hook script (the ones the proxy dispatcher calls directly) must be referenced in tools/HME/proxy/hook_bridge.js. Sub-dispatched scripts (posttooluse_addknowledge.sh, posttooluse_hme_*.sh, posttooluse_write.sh, posttooluse_agent.sh, pretooluse_check_pipeline.sh) are invoked from within other hooks based on CMD contents, not dispatched directly — they're excluded from this check and verified separately by the scripts that call them.
Every custom ESLint rule must be registered in eslint.config.mjs
project-rules.json must be valid JSON
invariants.json must be valid JSON (meta-invariant)
tools/HME/config/rag.json must be valid JSON (successor to the mcpServers.HME block in .mcp.json after the MCP decoupling)
feedback_graph.json must be valid JSON
adaptive-state.json must be valid JSON
Bias bounds manifest must exist for Phase 3 enforcement
globals.d.ts must exist for TypeScript ambient declarations
Evolution journal must exist for calibration anchors
Must have at least 23 custom ESLint rules
Must have at least 19 stutter variant files
l0Channels.js must define at least 25 channel constants
Stop hook must have all 8 critical enforcement sections
FAIL-scan middleware must exist in the proxy (moved from log-tool-call.sh after the MCP decoupling — log-tool-call.sh itself is now a stub)
_safety.sh must define all required helper functions
Every L0_CHANNELS constant must be referenced in at least one src/ file
Plugin hooks.json must be valid JSON
HTTP handlers + store modules must exist — absorbed into worker.py after the shim split (was hme_http.py pre-decoupling)
HTTP store module must exist (successor to parts of the old hme_http.py)
Pipeline main script must exist
NEXUS state helper must exist for edit tracking hooks
hooks/hooks.json must register hooks for all 7 lifecycle events
Every shell script in scripts/ must be executable
log/ directory must exist for LIFESAVER and error logging
tmp/ directory must exist for LIFESAVER watermarks and session state
Must have at least 5 run-history snapshots
7-layer coherence audit document must exist
KB Lance database must exist and contain at least one data file
Must have all 27 trust system pair assignments defined
All stutter variants must self-register via stutterVariants.register
Top 15 highest-caller IIFE globals must each have a KB entry
trace.jsonl and latest run-history snapshot must be from the same pipeline run (within 5 minutes)
All 11 known active coupling label pairs must be documented in ARCHITECTURE.md
ARCHITECTURE.md must document all three directionBias coupling sections
KB entries must not contain LLM thinking artifact tags
KB must have been updated within the last 14 days (staleness signal)
current-run.json unified pointer must be valid JSON
No untracked files exist outside .gitignore — every intentional file must be committed
hooks.json must route every event through _proxy_bridge.sh — the single entrypoint that forwards lifecycle events to the proxy-side dispatcher
CLAUDE.md must document the correct ESLint rule count (23)
feedback_graph.json must have at least 11 feedback loops
LIFESAVER.md enforcement reference must exist
_tab_helpers.sh must exist — sourced by posttooluse_addknowledge, posttooluse_agent, posttooluse_write
Every hook script must be documented in doc/HME.md hook table
_nexus.sh must define all 10 lifecycle state functions
_tab_helpers.sh must define all 4 compact tab functions

HME Python files must not use silent `except Exception: pass` catch-all (swallows all errors). Matches both same-line and next-line forms, project-wide under tools/HME/mcp/server. Promoted to error after the 149-hit backlog was cleared — any new instance is a regression.

HME Python files must not wrap `from hme_env import ENV` (or other infrastructure imports) in try/except fallback. Silent ImportError swallowed behind `os.environ.get(...)` fallback hid the synthesis_proxy_route ENV bug for weeks. Infrastructure imports must fail loud at module load time.

Hook scripts that emit Claude-facing briefings (KB CONSTRAINTS, AGENT PRIMER, ANTI-POLLING, etc.) must use `hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext` (on allow) or `permissionDecisionReason` (on deny), not `systemMessage` alone. systemMessage is user-terminal-only; a briefing hook that emits only systemMessage never reaches Claude — this silently gutted the entire pretooluse_edit briefing chain for months.

Shell hooks must not wrap `source <infrastructure-file>` in silent-swallow suffixes (`2>/dev/null || true`, `|| true`). The _safety.sh .env-path bug hid for months behind `source X 2>/dev/null || true` because the missing file failed invisibly, leaving PROJECT_ROOT unset in every hook. Infrastructure sources must fail loud or use explicit `[ -f X ] && source X` guard.
meta_layers.py must define trim helpers (_trim_narrative_file, _trim_counterfactuals_file) alongside its append-only file writes. Prevents unbounded JSONL growth.
OSError-only except handlers in operational_state.py must also catch ValueError (int/float conversion safety)
posttooluse_hme_review.sh must extract depth meter count and write REVIEW_ISSUES to nexus

posttooluse_hme_review.sh must parse the canonical HME_REVIEW_VERDICT marker that onboarding_chain.emit_review_verdict_marker() emits. Drift between the two caused clean reviews to fall into REVIEW_PARSE_FAILED until the hook regex was updated.
The server must emit verdicts via the canonical emit_review_verdict_marker() function. If this name is removed/renamed, the hook's contract regex will silently stop matching.

Claude CLI result.modelUsage[alias].contextWindow must be a number ≥1000 — routerClaude.ts:computeTurnUsage divides by this. If CLI version drift drops or renames the field, the context meter silently stalls and the chain mistriggers. The probe script skips when the CLI is unavailable or the API is unreachable.

scripts/hme-cli.js must prefix every stderr error line with 'hme-cli:' — three PostToolUse hooks (posttooluse_hme_read, posttooluse_hme_review, posttooluse_addknowledge) grep for this prefix to detect transport failures. Drift here silently turns 'worker down' into 'operation succeeded cleanly' in nexus state.

Every hook that consumes hme-cli output must grep for the '^hme-cli:' prefix to distinguish transport failure from content. Removing this check silently lets a failed CLI call masquerade as a successful one.
_nexus_pending() must check REVIEW_ISSUES count and block stop when count > 3

llama.cpp wall-clock enforcement belongs exclusively in llamacpp_daemon.py. No synthesis file may (a) spawn a thread and join(timeout=) on it, or (b) urlopen a llama-server inference URL (_LLAMACPP_ARBITER_URL / _LLAMACPP_CODER_URL) directly — all inference must route through _daemon_generate. Health/probe endpoints (/health, /api/ps) are exempt since they are not inference. _cancellable_urlopen is the preemption primitive and is exempt via marker.
register_todo_from_lifesaver must dedupe by text+source so a monitor loop cannot flood the todo store with zombie entries (116 such entries polluted todos.json before this rule landed).

HME Python files must not read os.environ directly — use hme_env.ENV.require/optional instead. os.environ is mutable by any subprocess and unauditable; .env on disk is the single source of truth. Subprocess env construction (os.environ.copy()) is exempt when marked with # env-ok. hme_env.py itself is exempt.
HME Python and shell files must not hardcode /home/jah — use PROJECT_ROOT from .env via hme_env.ENV or $PROJECT_ROOT in shell. Docs and logs are exempt.
invariants.shell: grep -rn '/home/jah' tools/HME/mcp/ tools/HME/hooks/ tools/HME/scripts/ tools/HME/activity/ tools/HME/proxy/ --include='*.py' --include='*.sh' --include='*.js' | grep -v '__pycache__' | grep -vE '\.log:|# path-ok'

invariants.description: HME server files must not hardcode llamacpp ports 11434/11435/11436 — they were retired in commit 0577c0f7 when local inference moved to llama-server. Use HME_LLAMACPP_ARBITER_URL / HME_LLAMACPP_CODER_URL via os.environ, or the env fallback in the legacy llamacpp branch guarded by HME_ARBITER_BACKEND.

Doubled fallback: `V.optionalFinite(x, N) || M` or `safePreBoot.call(fn, N) || M` is a dead outer fallback that also silently rewrites legitimate 0/false readings (see the regimeClassifierClassification 0→0.5 tension bug). Use the inner value directly, or nullish coalescing (??) if you want to catch only undefined.

HME Python exception handlers must bind to `as _var` or named var so the type/message can be logged. Unbound `except Exception:` paired with anything other than `pass` (which is separately forbidden) is a code smell — you probably want to log the failure. Exempt: `except Exception: pass` is caught by hme-py-no-silent-catchall above; this rule catches the `except Exception:` + block-without-name case.

Map/object histogram counter reads should use `?? 0` not `|| 0`. A zero count in `(counts.get(k) || 0) + 1` is coerced to 0 + 1 — correct here — but the idiom is brittle; `?? 0` documents intent and won't break if the counter type changes to allow falsy non-undefined values.
Phase 1 activity bridge (openshell_features_to_mimic.md) — shared event emitter CLI must exist so hooks can append to hme-activity.jsonl
Meta-coherence audit script (the detector-of-detectors) must exist so the HME diagnostic layer is continuously self-validated
_alias_subpackage must skip name==subpkg_name — aliasing a subpackage's own name to a .py module strips __path__, which breaks importlib.reload() for every submodule parent-lookup
activity_digest module must be in RELOADABLE so hot-reload picks up changes
status_unified must dispatch mode='activity' to activity_digest
Hooks that touch src/ or tools/HME/ edits must call tools/HME/activity/emit.py so the activity stream captures structural changes
invariants.shell: for f in tools/HME/hooks/posttooluse/posttooluse_edit.sh tools/HME/hooks/lifecycle/stop.sh tools/HME/hooks/pretooluse/pretooluse_edit.sh; do grep -qE 'activity/emit\.py|_emit_activity' "$f" || echo "$f missing activity emit"; done

invariants.description: Phase 2 (inference proxy) — hme_proxy.js must exist so ANTHROPIC_BASE_URL routing is available
hme_proxy.js must parse as valid Node so starting the daemon never fails at syntax level
Phase 3 (policy engine) — check-hme-coherence.js must exist as the pipeline gate reading hme-activity.jsonl
main-pipeline.js PRE_COMPOSITION must include check-hme-coherence so violations block composition
Phase 2.2 — KB staleness index builder must exist
Phase 2.3 — round coherence score computer must exist
main-pipeline.js POST_COMPOSITION must wire the staleness index + coherence score so they regenerate every run
Phase 2.4 — blind-spot surfacing module must exist under tools_analysis
Phase 2.5 — causal cascade analysis module must exist
trace_unified must dispatch mode='impact' to cascade_report
status_unified must dispatch the Phase 2.2–2.4 modes (staleness, coherence, blindspots)
Phase 2.1 jurisdiction injection must be wired in the proxy (distributed across hme_proxy.js / context.js / messages.js after the middleware refactor)
Phase 3.1 — hypothesis registry module must exist
learn_unified must dispatch hypothesize/hypothesis_test actions
posttooluse_edit.sh must split lazy vs productive violations using the staleness index
compute-coherence-score.js must include an exploration_bonus term from productive_incoherence events
Phase 3.3 — semantic drift checker + signature capture must exist
check-kb-semantic-drift.py must be present for the pipeline step to run
capture-kb-signatures.py must exist so baselines can be bootstrapped
Phase 3.4 — prediction reconciler must exist
cascade_analysis must log every prediction so the reconciler can score accuracy
Phase 3.5 — pattern crystallizer module must exist
learn_unified must dispatch action='crystallize'
status_unified must dispatch the Phase 3 modes (hypotheses, drift, accuracy, crystallized)
Proxy must inject open hypotheses and semantic drift warnings alongside jurisdiction context (Phase 3.1/3.3 — moved to context.js during the middleware split)
Phase 4.1 — musical ground truth correlator must exist (the external anchor)

hme_coherence must show non-degenerate variance across rounds. If it stays at a single value (usually 0) for many rounds, the read_coverage → coherence → musical_correlation → kb_trust cascade is silently broken. This invariant caught a months-long regression where the activity_log middleware emitted file_written events without the hme_read_prior field. min_distinct=3 because 0/1 outliers can occur even when upstream is broken — we need real motion to declare the cascade healthy.

EnCodec perceptual_complexity_avg must vary across rounds. If this is flat, the compositional output is genuinely identical every round OR the EnCodec pipeline is producing cached/stale signals. Either case means the external anchor's most-sensitive dimension is dead.

CLAP tension query peak must vary across rounds. Flat CLAP means the CLAP model isn't sensitive to the compositional motion the pipeline produces — either broken inference or the compositions are all semantically identical to CLAP.

hme_prediction_accuracy must show non-degenerate variance across rounds. Constant-zero accuracy means the cascade model predicts identically every time (dependency graph drifted, or reconciler never confirms).

Every pipeline_start must be followed by exactly one pipeline_run in the recent activity window. Catches observability regressions where a refactor drops emission of one of the paired events — the pipeline owns its own instrumentation, and a missing emission silently breaks every downstream slicer.

Every pipeline_run must be paired with a round_complete event in the recent window. These two events should always fire together (both emitted at pipeline finish); a mismatch means one of the two paths regressed. require_field=verdict filters out chat-turn round_complete markers from the pre-rename era.

file_written events must carry module names that look like real source file basenames — alphanumeric with underscore/dash/dot, 1-64 chars. Catches infrastructure leaks: LanceDB UUIDs, timestamps, hex strings that indicate the watcher filter is letting noise through.

Rounds with identical git SHA must produce hme_coherence values within 0.02 of each other. If the metric varies across identical-code runs, either a verifier has random inputs, a cache leaks state between runs, or something time-dependent is infecting the score.

At least 80% of RECENT file_written events (last 24h) should carry the 'source' field. If fewer, either the watcher (source=fs_watcher) or the pipeline (source=pipeline_script) has regressed recently — the require_fields filter on file-written-module-sane would silently pass by filtering most events instead of catching violations. Time-windowed (not event-windowed) because legacy events from before the source field was added shouldn't count.
invariants.shell: python3 -c "import json, os, time; p='metrics/hme-activity.jsonl'; lines=open(p).readlines() if os.path.isfile(p) else []; cutoff=time.time()-86400; fws=[json.loads(l) for l in lines[-2000:] if l.strip() and json.loads(l).get('event')=='file_written' and json.loads(l).get('ts',0)>=cutoff]; with_src=sum(1 for e in fws if e.get('source')); total=len(fws); print(f'recent file_written with source: {with_src}/{total} ({100*with_src//max(total,1)}%) — below 80% threshold') if total>=10 and 100*with_src/max(total,1) < 80 else None"

invariants.description: Once the predictor has processed 3+ rounds with >=1 shifted module each, recall should be >= 0.5 (predicted modules catch at least half of actual shifts). Low recall means the cascade model isn't seeing the dependency structure; low precision is acceptable (overprediction tolerable) but low recall is a structural blind spot.

Any legacy override with 0 fires AND 0 entries across the last 5 pipeline runs is a data-proven removal candidate. Fires at error severity after 5 zero rounds — by that point the signal is strong enough to demand action, not just warn. Escalation path: instrument (R11) → warn (3 rounds) → error (5 rounds). entropy-cap-0.19 was the first retirement via this pattern (R13).
invariants.shell: python3 -c "import json, os; p='metrics/legacy-override-history.jsonl'; rows=[json.loads(l) for l in open(p)] if os.path.isfile(p) else []; rows=rows[-5:]; ids=set(); [ids.update(r.get('fires',{}).keys()) for r in rows]; [ids.update(r.get('entries',{}).keys()) for r in rows]; zero=[i for i in ids if all(r.get('fires',{}).get(i,0)==0 and r.get('entries',{}).get(i,0)==0 for r in rows) and len(rows)>=5]; print('\n'.join('legacy override unused 5+ rounds — RETIRE: '+i for i in zero))"

invariants.description: Every LEGACY_OVERRIDES entry in check-hypermeta-jurisdiction.js must match at least one call site in SpecialCaps source. An id with zero matches means its detector regex drifted (variable renamed, refactored out, etc.) — silently disarming the jurisdiction gate. registeredByLegacyId must have a positive count for every allowlisted id.
invariants.shell: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('metrics/hypermeta-jurisdiction.json')); reg=d.get('registeredByLegacyId',{}); legacy=d.get('legacyOverrides',[]); missing=[l['id'] for l in legacy if reg.get(l['id'],0)==0]; print('\n'.join('legacy id has zero matches: '+m for m in missing))"

invariants.description: Meta-invariant: another invariant must not have been FAILing for ≥10 consecutive runs. Catches the meta-pattern where alarms become background noise and never get addressed.

The ultimate anti-drift check: hme_coherence must be at least WEAKLY positively correlated with the musical verdict, not flat and not inverse. A strongly negative correlation means HME's process discipline is actively hurting the music — the whole optimization direction is wrong. This invariant is INERT when the correlation is degenerate (that's covered by coherence-has-variance); it only fires once there's enough signal to judge direction.
invariants.name: hme_coherence__verdict_numeric

invariants.description: Every round snapshot must have a round_id so downstream analyzers (intention-gap, reflexivity, trajectory) can cross-reference. Anonymous rounds collapse into unordered history. allow_null=false because null means 'no identity at all' here, not 'cold start' — identity should be present on every single round.
Phase 4.2 — KB trust weight computer must exist
Phase 4.3 — intention-execution gap tracker must exist
Phase 4.4 — HME self-audit module must exist
Phase 4.5 — adversarial probe generator must exist
main-pipeline.js POST_COMPOSITION must run all 3 Phase 4 scripts
status_unified must dispatch the Phase 4 modes (music_truth, kb_trust, intention_gap, self_audit, probes)
Phase 5.1 — compositional trajectory script must exist
Phase 5.2 — coherence budget script must exist (homeostatic governance vs. maximization)
Phase 5.3 — architectural negative space discovery module must exist
Phase 5.4 — Evolver cognitive load modeling module must exist
Phase 5.5 — human ground truth module must exist
main-pipeline.js POST_COMPOSITION must run Phase 5 scripts (trajectory + budget)
status_unified must dispatch the Phase 5 modes (trajectory, budget, negative_space, cognitive_load, ground_truth)
learn_unified must dispatch action='ground_truth'
compute-kb-trust-weights.py must unconditionally tier human_ground_truth KB entries as HIGH
Phase 6.1 — cascade_analysis._log_prediction must carry an injected flag
reconcile-predictions.js must split predictions into clean vs injected buckets
Phase 6.2 — constitutional identity derivation script must exist
Phase 6.3 — doc drift detector must exist
Phase 6.4 — generalization extractor must exist
Phase 6.5 — multi-agent observability scaffold must exist
Phase 6 reports module (constitution/doc_drift/generalizations/reflexivity) must exist
main-pipeline.js POST_COMPOSITION must run Phase 6 scripts
status_unified must dispatch the Phase 6 modes (constitution, doc_drift, generalizations, reflexivity, multi_agent)
HME Python files must use ENV (from hme_env) not raw os.environ.get for config. hme_env.py itself is exempt.
invariants.shell: grep -rnE 'os\.environ\.get\(' tools/HME/mcp/ --include='*.py' | grep -v hme_env.py | grep -v '__pycache__' | grep -v '\.log' | grep -v '\.jsonl' | grep -vE '^\s*#' | grep -v 'env\[.*\] = os.environ' | grep -v '# env-ok'

invariants.description: index_directory must take zero arguments everywhere — ragIndexDirs in tools/HME/config/rag.json is the ONLY source of indexable paths. Any signature with a directory/path parameter is a bypass vector that WILL be abused as a fallback. See the walk_code_files purge in commit 469d072b.

indexing_mode.py and tools_index.py must NEVER import SentenceTransformer or torch — they talk to the daemon via HTTP, which tells the shim what device to use. Direct model loading in these files bypasses the daemon's GPU allocation authority. See the 'catastrophic failure' fix where indexing_mode was loading models directly.

log/, metrics/, and tmp/ exist ONLY at the project root. Any subdirectory with those names is a duplicate that fragments telemetry (see the log-tool-call.sh $CWD-as-PROJECT_ROOT bug that silently spawned 10+ orphan log/ dirs). Writers must use $PROJECT_ROOT from .env; relative paths resolved against $(pwd) are banned in hook/daemon contexts. If a subsystem wants its own logs, route through $PROJECT_ROOT/log/<subsystem>-*.log instead of a sibling directory.

Only llamacpp_daemon.py and rag_engines.py may reference CUDA device strings for model placement. Other HME files must not contain 'cuda:0' or 'cuda:1' device assignments — they route through the daemon which owns GPU topology. rag_engine/engine.py is exempt (fallback device picker for non-shim mode). # device-ok comments exempt specific lines.
invariants.shell: grep -rnE '"cuda:[0-9]"' tools/HME/mcp/ --include='*.py' | grep -v '__pycache__' | grep -v llamacpp_daemon.py | grep -v rag_engines.py | grep -v 'rag_engine/engine.py' | grep -v vram_manager.py | grep -v '# device-ok'

invariants.description: emit_review_verdict_marker must be importable from server.onboarding_chain AND produce a string containing HME_REVIEW_VERDICT for the 'clean' verdict. Guards against signature drift that the pattern-in-file check can't catch — the function must actually work, not just exist.
invariants.shell: python3 -c "import sys; sys.path.insert(0,'tools/HME/mcp'); from server.onboarding_chain import emit_review_verdict_marker; m=emit_review_verdict_marker('clean'); assert 'HME_REVIEW_VERDICT' in m and 'clean' in m, f'bad marker: {m!r}'" 2>&1 | grep -v '^$'

invariants.description: tools/HME/KB/code_chunks.lance/_deletions/ must have at most 50 files. Above this threshold, dead arrow files slow table opens and waste disk. Run `python3 scripts/compact-lance-tables.py` to compact. This count grows on every re-index cycle (delete old chunks + add new) and needs periodic compaction.
invariants.shell: python3 -c "import os, sys; d='tools/HME/KB/code_chunks.lance/_deletions'; n=len([f for f in os.listdir(d) if os.path.isfile(os.path.join(d,f))]) if os.path.isdir(d) else 0; sys.stdout.write(f'_deletions has {n} files (cap=50)\n') if n > 50 else None"

invariants.description: scripts/hme-cli.js must declare a CLI_VERSION constant and emit it in --version output. Allows version mismatch detection between old wrappers and new worker deployments.
tools/HME/mcp/worker.py must expose a GET /version endpoint. Paired with CLI_VERSION in hme-cli.js so deployment mismatches (old wrapper, new worker) are detectable.

KB lance tables (tools/HME/KB/*.lance/) are ephemeral generated state — regenerated by rag_engines indexing and compacted by scripts/compact-lance-tables.py. Their _deletions/, _transactions/, _versions/, and data/ internals must not be tracked by git. Tracking them produces gigabytes of binary drift per indexer run and breaks patch-based commit tools (GitLens) with 'cannot apply binary patch without full index line' errors. If this invariant fails, the fix is: add to .gitignore, then `git rm --cached -r tools/HME/KB/<table>.lance`.

Every strong-negative correlation (r <= -0.4) in metrics/trace.jsonl must be accounted for in metrics/hme-suspected-upstreams.json as candidate, confirmed, or refuted. An unaccounted negative correlation means the system is observing a tension it hasn't named — per the antagonism-bridge principle, that's a missing design artifact. See doc/ANTAGONISM_BRIDGES.md.
tools/HME/config/invariants.json and metrics/hme-suspected-upstreams.json both parse and the suspected-upstreams registry has the required three buckets (candidates/confirmed/refuted).
invariants.shell: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('metrics/hme-suspected-upstreams.json')); assert all(k in d for k in ('candidates','confirmed','refuted')), 'missing bucket'" 2>&1 | grep -v '^$'

invariants.description: All three wire-version components (cli/proxy/worker) must read from tools/HME/config/versions.json — not hardcoded strings. Protects the three-way version check from typo-drift.
tools/HME/config/versions.json is the single source of truth for cli/proxy/worker wire versions. Must be valid JSON with all three keys.
invariants.shell: python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('tools/HME/config/versions.json')); assert 'cli' in d and 'proxy' in d and 'worker' in d, 'missing version key'" 2>&1 | grep -v '^$'

invariants.description: tools/HME/proxy/hme_proxy.js must declare PROXY_VERSION and expose GET /version. Completes the three-way (cli/proxy/worker) version check surfaced via `hme-cli --version` — a stale proxy between a new CLI and new worker is otherwise invisible.

Every sketch in lab/sketches.js must declare a patches: [] array listing globals it monkey-patches in postBoot(). Enforced by lab/run.js at sketch load time. Ensures patches are declared, auditable, and cleaned up between sketches.
